sub_1_6dfa43.title: Origins of the East India Company
sub_1_6dfa43.objective: Understand the historical context leading to the founding of the East India Company.
key_facts: The late 16th century saw increased European exploration and the search for new trade routes.
England was keen to compete with other European powers like Spain and Portugal in overseas trade.
The East India Company was established to capitalize on the lucrative spice trade.
sub_1_6dfa43.assessment_prompt: What were the main motivations for England to establish the East India Company?
sub_1_6dfa43.research_raw_text:
📚 VERIFIED FACTS (Source: WIKIPEDIA):
Confidence: HIGH

The East India Company (EIC) was an English, and later British, joint-stock company that was founded in 1600 and dissolved in 1874. It was formed to trade in the Indian Ocean region, initially with the East Indies, and later with East Asia. The company gained control of large parts of the Indian subcontinent and Hong Kong. At its peak, the company was the largest corporation in the world by various measures and had its own armed forces in the form of the company's three presidency armies, totalling about 260,000 soldiers, twice the size of the British Army at certain times.
Source URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_India_Company

sub_1_6dfa43.title: Founding of the East India Company
sub_1_6dfa43.objective: Detail the founding process of the East India Company in 1600.
key_facts: The East India Company was founded by a royal charter granted by Queen Elizabeth I on December 31, 1600.
It was established as a joint-stock company, allowing investors to pool resources and share risks.
The charter granted the company a monopoly on English trade with the East Indies.
sub_1_6dfa43.assessment_prompt: What was the significance of the royal charter granted to the East India Company?

sub_1_6dfa43.title: Charter of 1600: Key Provisions
sub_1_6dfa43.objective: Identify and understand the key provisions of the East India Company's charter.
key_facts: The charter granted the company exclusive rights to trade in the East Indies for 15 years.
It allowed the company to govern itself, appointing its own officials and making its own rules.
The company was authorized to mint its own currency and negotiate treaties with local rulers.
sub_1_6dfa43.assessment_prompt: List three key provisions of the East India Company's charter.

sub_1_6dfa43.title: Impact of the Company's Founding
sub_1_6dfa43.objective: Assess the initial impact of the East India Company's founding on global trade.
key_facts: The company's founding marked the beginning of British colonial expansion in Asia.
It played a significant role in global trade, particularly in spices, textiles, and tea.
The company's success contributed to the rise of Britain as a global economic power.
sub_1_6dfa43.assessment_prompt: How did the founding of the East India Company influence global trade?
